key_insight: Machine learning can accurately predict friction stir welding joint strength, with Random Forest Regression and Artificial Neural Networks performing best and SHAP revealing the most influential process parameters.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, data-driven process optimization for welding setups, especially when balancing strength in similar and dissimilar joints.
method: Trained and tuned multiple ML models: Random Forest, ANN, KNN, SVR
Used SHAP to rank feature importance and interpret parameter effects
evidence_signal: Across the collected datasets, Random Forest Regression and ANN achieved the highest prediction accuracy, and SHAP identified tool rotational speed and plate thickness as the dominant variables.
watch_item: Because the dataset is assembled from peer-reviewed literature, model transferability may be limited by differences in materials, tooling, and reporting conditions across studies.
paper_title: Data-driven prediction of joint strength in friction stir welding: A machine learning and feature sensitivity analysis
